summary: The 'q1' dataset context provides uniform issue counts (100 per problem across q1-q10) but lacks differentiating metadata such as issue status, resolution progress, or activity timestamps. Consequently, no problem can be distinguished by traction, solvability, or proof completion from the data provided, and any prioritization below is provisional pending richer signals.
problems: All ten problems (q1-q10) report an identical 100 issues each, which suggests a placeholder or capped count rather than genuine differential activity.
No proof status breakdown (open/closed/in-progress) is available, making it impossible to assess true progress or near-term solvability.
Missing per-issue metadata (severity, type, last-updated) prevents identification of common patterns or recurring blockers.
The flat distribution offers no basis to rank problems by traction or research momentum.
highlights: A complete and consistent issue inventory exists for every problem, providing a clean baseline for future tracking.
Uniform coverage means no problem has been neglected in issue logging.
The dataset is well-structured for instrumentation once richer status fields are added.

suggested_todos.title: Augment issue records with status fields (open/in-progress/resolved) to enable real progress tracking
Add timestamps and activity logs to surface which problems have genuine recent traction
Investigate why all problems report exactly 100 issues (possible cap or placeholder)
Tag issues by category/severity to reveal common patterns across problems
Define solvability heuristics so near-term completion candidates can be flagged automatically
